project.name: pbtracker
project.description: A computer vision pipeline for tracking players and the ball in pickleball match footage. Built with YOLO object detection, OpenCV geometric filtering, and deep learning-based person re-identification.

nodes.name: manual_court_selector.py
nodes.summary: Provides a manual court selector GUI using OpenCV to define and register the 12 key points of a pickleball court.

nodes.name: ManualCourtDetector
nodes.summary: Detector class that allows manual annotation of 12 court keypoints on a video frame and saves them for later tracking overlay.

nodes.name: ManualCourtDetector.get_keypoints
nodes.summary: Initializes an OpenCV window to collect 12 keypoint locations from user mouse clicks, with stub file loading/saving support.

nodes.name: ManualCourtDetector.draw_keypoints_on_video
nodes.summary: Annotates keypoints as labeled circles on a list of video frames for visual confirmation.

nodes.name: main.py
nodes.summary: The main orchestrator and execution entry point of the pickleball tracking and telemetry video processing pipeline.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Initializes detectors/trackers, runs the player and ball detection loops (utilizing stub caches), computes telemetry and officiating calls, and renders the annotated video HUD output.

nodes.name: ball_tracker.py
nodes.summary: Implements the ball tracking system using a YOLO v8/v5 model and Pandas-based interpolation to estimate missing frames.

nodes.name: BallTracker
nodes.summary: A tracker class responsible for detecting the pickleball across frames and smoothing trajectories using interpolation.

nodes.name: BallTracker.interpolate_ball_positions
nodes.summary: Interpolates missing ball positions in a sequence using linear interpolation and backward fill to handle detection flickering.

nodes.name: BallTracker.detect_frames
nodes.summary: Performs YOLO inference on a sequence of frames to locate the ball, with optional caching via pickle stub files.

nodes.name: BallTracker.draw_bboxes
nodes.summary: Annotates the ball bounding boxes and center dots onto frames, using green for detected and pink for interpolated coordinates.

nodes.name: auto_player_filter.py
nodes.summary: Performs automatic player isolation, spectator purging, and Re-ID mapping using L2-normalized templates and quadrant sorting.

nodes.name: expand_polygon_asymmetric
nodes.summary: Asymmetrically expands a court bounding polygon to include out-of-bounds player positions relative to baseline heights.

nodes.name: AutoPlayerFilter
nodes.summary: Filters raw player track detections, purges off-court spectators, and maps remaining tracks to Master IDs 1-4 using appearance embeddings.

nodes.name: AutoPlayerFilter.run_filtration
nodes.summary: Executes a seeding pass to lock player template embeddings and a matching pass to filter and align all frames to Master IDs.

nodes.name: ball_physics_analyzer.py
nodes.summary: Analyzes ball trajectory, velocity, and acceleration to detect physics events (bounces and paddle hits) with temporal cooldown constraints.

nodes.name: BallPhysicsAnalyzer
nodes.summary: Analyzer class that processes ball coordinate stubs to calculate kinematics and identify court bounces and paddle hits.

nodes.name: BallPhysicsAnalyzer.load_and_preprocess
nodes.summary: Loads ball tracking stubs, interpolates missing coordinates, and applies a rolling mean window filter to smooth pixel jitter.

nodes.name: BallPhysicsAnalyzer.compute_kinematics
nodes.summary: Computes velocity components, acceleration components, and their magnitudes from smoothed coordinates.

nodes.name: BallPhysicsAnalyzer.detect_events
nodes.summary: Detects court floor bounces and paddle hits based on acceleration magnitude inflections and velocity sign changes with temporal lockout.

nodes.name: app.py
nodes.summary: Main Streamlit application that renders the InBound Vision dashboard with match performance statistics, player scorecards, rally logs, and interactive 2D court telemetry plots.

nodes.name: get_video_metadata
nodes.summary: Scans the input_videos directory and dynamically resolves the video dimensions, framerate, and total frames using OpenCV.

nodes.name: load_datasets
nodes.summary: Loads pickle stubs for tracking data and aggregates locomotion statistics, speed calculations, and proximity-based shot attribution for all players.

nodes.name: load_rally_log
nodes.summary: Segments match plays into discrete rallies using a temporal activity gap threshold and compiles shot and bounce analytics per rally.

nodes.name: mini_court.py
nodes.summary: Defines the MiniCourt class, which uses homography transformation to project 2D camera coordinates onto a top-down, real-world representation of a Pickleball court.

nodes.name: MiniCourt
nodes.summary: Top-down 2D mapping manager that computes homography matrices, transforms pixel coordinates to court-space feet, and draws players, ball, and officiating calls on a minimap canvas.

nodes.name: MiniCourt.__init__
nodes.summary: Initializes the top-down canvas dimensions, official pickleball boundaries in feet, and triggers homography matrix loading.

nodes.name: MiniCourt.load_homography_matrix
nodes.summary: Loads pre-defined court keypoints from a pickle file and computes the 3x3 homography matrix mapping image space to physical court dimensions.

nodes.name: MiniCourt.draw_mini_court_base
nodes.summary: Renders the static boundaries of the pickleball court, including the kitchen line, center line, and net line, onto a dark background canvas.

nodes.name: MiniCourt.transform_point
nodes.summary: Applies perspective transformation to project 2D image coordinates to physical ground coordinates in feet.

nodes.name: MiniCourt.draw_points_on_mini_court
nodes.summary: Draws players, the ball (interpolated or detected), and recent bounce line calls (IN/OUT) onto the mini-court minimap canvas.

nodes.name: officiating_engine.py
nodes.summary: Provides the OfficiatingEngine class, which detects when a ball bounce is IN or OUT and overlays a broadcast-style banner with the call onto the video frame.

nodes.name: OfficiatingEngine
nodes.summary: Officiating manager class that computes line calls for bounce events and handles visual overlays of officiating calls.

nodes.name: OfficiatingEngine.get_line_calls
nodes.summary: Iterates over ball events to identify bounce frames, converts the bounce coordinates to physical court dimensions, and determines if the bounce was IN or OUT.

nodes.name: OfficiatingEngine.draw_calls_on_video
nodes.summary: Applies a translucent dark banner and large BGR text overlays ("IN" in green, "OUT" in red) onto video frames for bounce occurrences.

nodes.name: stats_telemetry_tracker.py
nodes.summary: Provides the StatsTelemetryTracker class, which records and computes locomotion telemetry (speed, distance) and proximity-based hit attribution for individual players, and draws a HUD overlay.

nodes.name: StatsTelemetryTracker
nodes.summary: Performance metrics tracker class that aggregates match locomotion data, attributes hits to players, and maintains a historical snapshot of player metrics.

nodes.name: StatsTelemetryTracker.__init__
nodes.summary: Initializes telemetry tracker settings, instantiates a MiniCourt module, and seeds empty stat structures for players 1 to 4.

nodes.name: StatsTelemetryTracker.process_match_telemetry
nodes.summary: Processes tracking coordinates frame-by-frame to compute distances and instantaneous speed, and detects player hits using ball-proximity measurements.

nodes.name: StatsTelemetryTracker.draw_stats_hud
nodes.summary: Draws a premium semi-transparent overlay HUD with colored indicators displaying individual player performance stats on the video frames.

nodes.name: METHODS_AND_RETROSPECTIVE.md
nodes.summary: Detailed documentation of the pbtracker technology stack, key geometric and kinematic methods, and a retrospective analysis of failed and successful tracking attempts.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Main project documentation detailing the pickleball tracking pipeline, directory structure, quick start installation, post-processing tools, and cached data formats.

nodes.name: graphify.html
nodes.summary: Interactive HTML dashboard displaying a Canvas-based force-directed architecture graph of the pbtracker modules, dependencies, and data flow relationships.
nodes.languageNotes: Single-file interactive HTML/CSS/JavaScript bundle rendering a Canvas-based force-directed graph.

nodes.name: pyproject.toml
nodes.summary: Python project configuration file defining dependencies such as boxmot, lapx, opencv-python, streamlit, and ultralytics for the pbtracker project.

nodes.name: .python-version
nodes.summary: Specifies the target Python version (3.12) required for the project environment.

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Excludes temporary cache, locks, data files, and video assets from project-wide analysis.

nodes.name: config.json
nodes.summary: Configures localization settings for the understanding-anything plugin, setting output language to English.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Initialization script for the court line detection package.

nodes.name: analyze_filtration.py
nodes.summary: Analyzes raw player tracking stubs and court boundaries to evaluate spatial filtration and kinetic movement threshold filters.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Calculates track spatial constraints and kinetic movement to print a detailed filtration analysis report.

nodes.name: analyze_std.py
nodes.summary: Computes spatial coordinate standard deviation and range to determine track dispersion and movement patterns.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Calculates standard deviation of player foot positions to output tracking statistics.

nodes.name: analyze_stubs.py
nodes.summary: Calculates and prints statistics (such as lifespans, coordinate ranges, and on-court metrics) from player detections pickle stubs.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Reads player and court keypoint stubs to evaluate track ID lifespans and court boundary coverage.

nodes.name: annotate_frames_debug.py
nodes.summary: Annotates specific frames of the input video with court keypoints and player bounding boxes to verify detection accuracy.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Draws keypoints and player bounding boxes on selected frames and saves annotated images to a debug directory.

nodes.name: botsort_custom.yaml
nodes.summary: Custom tracker parameters configuration specifying threshold limits and buffers for BoT-SORT.

nodes.name: check_malaysia.py
nodes.summary: Checks and displays the total number of frames in the Malaysia video file.

nodes.name: check_video_frames.py
nodes.summary: Retrieves and prints total frames, dimensions, and frame rate for various input video files.

nodes.name: extract_all_raw_crops.py
nodes.summary: Extracts and saves image crops for player track IDs with a lifespan exceeding 100 frames to inspect detection consistency.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Processes video frames, crops out player bounding boxes, and saves those with high track lifetimes.

nodes.name: extract_final_frames.py
nodes.summary: Extracts specific frames from the final annotated tracking video for visual verification.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Saves specified frames from the final output video to verify overlay annotations.

nodes.name: extract_fine_tuned_frames.py
nodes.summary: Extracts selected frames from tracking output videos to check physics-based trajectory and bounding box overlays.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Reads video frames and saves frames representing specific index milestones.

nodes.name: extract_malaysia_frames.py
nodes.summary: Extracts designated verification frames from annotated Malaysia match video outputs.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Saves specific frames from the annotated Malaysia match video to inspect tracking overlays.

nodes.name: extract_newvideo_frames.py
nodes.summary: Extracts specific frame indices from annotated newvideo output files to check tracking consistency.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Saves designated frames from newvideo outputs to a debugging directory.

nodes.name: extract_player_crops.py
nodes.summary: Extracts the first occurrence crop of player IDs 1, 2, 3, and 4 from the newclip video for verification.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Iterates through frames using player tracking stubs to crop and save player bounding boxes.

nodes.name: generate_raw_tracks.py
nodes.summary: Processes the input video to perform player tracking using PlayerTracker and saves raw, unfiltered detections to a pickle stub.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Initializes PlayerTracker, runs tracking on video frames, dumps raw track pickle files, and prints track statistics.

nodes.name: inspect_malaysia_players.py
nodes.summary: Inspects player tracking detections for the Malaysia match, displaying presence and bounding box statistics for each player ID.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Loads Malaysia player tracking stubs and prints first/last frames and mean bounding boxes.

nodes.name: prepare_newvideo_stubs.py
nodes.summary: Copies active court, player, and ball detection stubs to newvideo-specific stub names for testing.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Copies court keypoint, player, and ball detection files to testing stub names.

nodes.name: print_frame_0.py
nodes.summary: Utility script to load player detections and print frame 0 player bounding box values.

nodes.name: rename_stubs.py
nodes.summary: Renames newvideo-specific tracking stubs to Malaysia tracking stubs to prep test data.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Renames temporary newvideo stubs to Malaysia match suffixes.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Initialization file for the trackers package.

nodes.name: player_tracker.py
nodes.summary: Core player tracker module implementing YOLO object detection and BoxMOT tracker updates with OSNet ReID embedding extraction.

nodes.name: expand_polygon_asymmetric
nodes.summary: Asymmetrically scales court polygon coordinate boundaries near vs. far baseline to improve tracking search boundaries.

nodes.name: PlayerTracker
nodes.summary: Wraps YOLO model inference and BoxMOT tracker state updates, featuring court-boundary validation and ReID embedding matching.

nodes.name: __init__
nodes.summary: Initializes PlayerTracker state, loading the YOLO model and instantiating BoT-SORT with OSNet ReID weights on designated device.

nodes.name: detect_frames
nodes.summary: Performs player detection and tracking on a batch of video frames, performing court boundary checks and ReID feature extraction.

nodes.name: draw_bboxes
nodes.summary: Annotates frames with bounding boxes, colors based on court status, and Player labels.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Initialization file for the utilities package.

nodes.name: check_ids.py
nodes.summary: Analyzes the player detection tracking stub file to generate and print a usage summary sorted by frame presence.

nodes.name: generate_id_debug_frame.py
nodes.summary: Generates a single annotated diagnostic image from a source video highlighting detected players and their associated tracking IDs.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Main execution logic that parses CLI arguments, loads tracking data, seeks to a populated frame, annotates it with bounding boxes/IDs, and saves it.

nodes.name: tracklet_merger.py
nodes.summary: Post-processing script to heal broken or fragmented tracklets by correlating them using temporal, spatial, and HSV color similarity.

nodes.name: crop_player
nodes.summary: Extracts and clamps a bounding-box region of interest representing a player from a video frame.

nodes.name: build_tracklet_segments
nodes.summary: Parses detection frames to build lifespan metadata (start, end, first/last bbox) for each unique track ID.

nodes.name: find_merge_candidates
nodes.summary: Identifies pairs of candidate tracklets that satisfy temporal window and spatial centroid distance limits.

nodes.name: score_visual_similarity
nodes.summary: Extracts crops from the video frames for candidate tracklets and computes their HSV color histogram correlation score.

nodes.name: apply_remap
nodes.summary: Applies a track ID remap dictionary to the player detection timeline, handling any frame collisions.

nodes.name: run_merger
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the post-processing merge workflow: loads stubs, filters candidates, scores visual similarity, applies remaps, and saves output.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Parses command-line arguments and invokes the run_merger pipeline.

nodes.name: deep_reid_healer.py
nodes.summary: Post-processing track stitcher that leverages a headless ResNet50 model to extract deep visual embeddings from player crops and stitches fragmented tracklets via cosine similarity.
nodes.languageNotes: Leverages PyTorch and torchvision's ResNet50 to compute 2048-dimensional normalized embeddings for visual comparison.

nodes.name: get_device
nodes.summary: Detects and returns the best available PyTorch compute device, preferring CUDA or Apple Metal (MPS) over CPU fallback.

nodes.name: DeepReIDExtractor
nodes.summary: Wraps a headless pretrained ResNet50 convolutional neural network to transform player image crops into high-dimensional visual embedding vectors.

nodes.name: __init__
nodes.summary: Initializes the DeepReIDExtractor class, setting up the torchvision normalization transforms and loading the headless ResNet50 backbone.

nodes.name: extract
nodes.summary: Extracts an L2-normalized 2048-dimensional feature embedding vector from an input BGR crop image.

nodes.name: safe_crop
nodes.summary: Clamps a bounding box to frame dimensions and extracts the crop from the video frame.

nodes.name: segment_tracks
nodes.summary: Builds a dictionary containing metadata, bounding boxes, and frame indices for each unique track ID.

nodes.name: build_baseline_profiles
nodes.summary: Extracts and averages embeddings from early frames to establish ground-truth baseline visual identity profiles for master tracks.

nodes.name: match_fragments
nodes.summary: Identifies late-appearing fragment tracklets and scores them against baseline profiles using cosine similarity to find re-ID matches.

nodes.name: apply_remap_and_drop
nodes.summary: Applies identity remapping to fragments and filters out designated noise track IDs across all detection frames.

nodes.name: print_track_summary
nodes.summary: Prints a formatted log table summarizing all track IDs, their frame spans, and detection frame counts.

nodes.name: run_healer
nodes.summary: Runs the complete Re-ID healing pipeline from loading pickle data, extracting baseline and fragment embeddings, remapping, and saving.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Parses CLI options and invokes the run_healer pipeline with the selected configuration.

layers.name: Presentation Layer
layers.description: Handles dashboard UI rendering and interactive visualizations.

layers.name: Application Orchestrator
layers.description: Acts as the execution entry point and orchestrates the video processing pipeline.

layers.name: Computer Vision Trackers
layers.description: Implements player and ball tracking systems using YOLO object detection models.

layers.name: Court Geometry & Mapping
layers.description: Handles homography transformations, perspective projection, and court selector GUI.

layers.name: Officiating & Telemetry Engine
layers.description: Processes kinematics, attributions, line calls, and HUD telemetry overlays.

layers.name: Data Healing & Re-ID
layers.description: Post-processes and stitches fragmented player tracklets using visual embedding models.

layers.name: Diagnostics & Scratchpad
layers.description: Temporary scripts, one-off analyses, data checks, and frame annotation debug utilities.

layers.name: Project Infrastructure & Config
layers.description: Configuration files, ignore lists, dependency definitions, and project documentation.

tour.title: Project Overview & Structure
tour.description: Start with the README to understand the pbtracker project goals: a computer vision pipeline that tracks players and the ball in pickleball match videos. It explains the directory layout, quick start setup, and 5 sequential pipeline steps.

tour.title: Project Settings and Environment Setup
tour.description: Look at pyproject.toml and .python-version to understand the project configuration and target environment (Python 3.12). It defines critical dependencies such as boxmot (BoT-SORT tracker), lapx, opencv-python, streamlit, and ultralytics (YOLOv8).

tour.title: Pipeline Orchestrator Entry Point
tour.description: The main.py file acts as the primary execution engine. It defines the main() orchestration loop which initializes tracking models, coordinates frame iteration, loads/saves detection caches (stubs), runs physics and officiating analysis, and renders the annotated video output.

tour.title: Interactive Court Boundary Selection
tour.description: The manual_court_selector.py script provides an interactive OpenCV GUI tool. It runs on the first frame of a match video, requiring the user to click exactly 12 court boundary keypoints. These coordinates are cached to verify on-court containment and project coordinates to physical space.

tour.title: Multi-Player Detection and Tracking
tour.description: The player_tracker.py module is responsible for loading the pre-trained YOLOv8x model and tracking players across video frames. It uses expand_polygon_asymmetric() to expand the court boundary polygon, verifying if players are actively on-court or spectators off-court.

tour.title: Pickleball Trajectory Tracking & Interpolation
tour.description: The ball_tracker.py module uses a fine-tuned YOLOv5 model to detect the pickleball. Since ball detection often flickers due to fast movement and occlusion, it uses interpolate_ball_positions() which applies Pandas linear interpolation and back-filling to estimate missing frames.

tour.title: Top-Down Court Projection (Homography)
tour.description: The mini_court.py module performs top-down 2D court mapping. It uses OpenCV to calculate a 3x3 homography matrix mapping perspective-distorted pixel points to physical ground-level coordinates in feet. This is crucial for accurate kinematics and bounce localization.

tour.title: Ball Physics & Kinematics Engine
tour.description: The ball_physics_analyzer.py module processes ball coordinates over time. It computes instantaneous velocity and acceleration components, using change points and temporal cooldown locks to automatically detect paddle hits and court floor bounces.

tour.title: Officiating & Line Boundary Decisions
tour.description: The officiating_engine.py module handles automated referee calls. It maps court bounces detected by the physics analyzer to the physical court boundary lines. It determines if a bounce is IN or OUT and overlays large colored banners on the video stream.

tour.title: Kinematic Telemetry & HUD Overlays
tour.description: The stats_telemetry_tracker.py module calculates locomotion statistics for players, including running speed and total distance covered. It also detects paddle hits based on player-to-ball proximity and draws the top HUD overlay showing stats for each player.

tour.title: Spatio-Temporal & HSV Track Stitching
tour.description: The tracklet_merger.py script acts as the first-level track healer. It resolves fragmented tracker IDs by analyzing spatial centroid distance, temporal gaps, and visual similarity of players (computed via OpenCV HSV color histogram correlation).

tour.title: Deep Neural Re-ID Identity Healing
tour.description: The deep_reid_healer.py module runs deep learning-based identity recovery. It extracts 2048-dimensional appearance embeddings from player crops using a PyTorch ResNet50 model, matching late-appearing fragment tracks to baseline master profiles via cosine similarity.

tour.title: Interactive Performance Dashboard
tour.description: The app.py script mounts a Streamlit dashboard. It loads the cached player locomotion metrics, physics events, and rally logs to render interactive movement heatmaps, speed over time plots, and game summaries using Plotly visualizations.

tour.title: Project Methodology & Algorithm Analysis
tour.description: End the tour with METHODS_AND_RETROSPECTIVE.md. It documents the mathematical and geometrical formulas behind court homography, coordinate mapping, physics thresholding, and details failed tracking approaches and future improvements.
